cases.case: [rich] CONTINUITY: behaviourally equivalent systems are never called DIFFERENT
[rich] DIFFERENCE: systems with different function or hidden state SEPARATE
[rich] the continuity and difference regions do not overlap (a real gap)
cases.detail: max continuity 0.0000 < min difference 0.0301; radii r_c=0.0075, r_s=0.0226

cases.case: [rich] a REDUNDANT TAP creates no fingerprint difference (three taps, two causes)
[rich] hidden state is separated from a LATCH (same parts, different behaviour)
cases.detail: reg_delay CONTAINS a register and is not a state machine; toggle is. d=0.3302

cases.case: [rich] RICH access RESCUES a system the droplet repertoire cannot interrogate at all
cases.detail: OR_bit1 responsiveness = 0.08 under rich access (0.00 under the droplet repertoire): unclamping its register makes it speak

cases.case: [droplet] CONTINUITY: behaviourally equivalent systems are never called DIFFERENT
[droplet] DIFFERENCE: systems with different function or hidden state SEPARATE
[droplet] the continuity and difference regions do not overlap (a real gap)
cases.detail: max continuity 0.0000 < min difference 0.0602; radii r_c=0.015, r_s=0.0451

cases.case: [droplet] a REDUNDANT TAP creates no fingerprint difference (three taps, two causes)
[droplet] hidden state is separated from a LATCH (same parts, different behaviour)
cases.detail: reg_delay CONTAINS a register and is not a state machine; toggle is. d=0.4115

cases.case: [droplet] a SILENT system is INDETERMINATE, never matched
cases.detail: OR_bit1 responsiveness = 0.00: a saturated gate answers nothing to any admissible droplet probe

cases.case: C1 systems distinguishable under RICH access are INDISTINGUISHABLE under the droplet repertoire
cases.detail: rich=['DIFFERENT', 'DIFFERENT', 'DIFFERENT', 'DIFFERENT']  droplet=['INDISTINGUISHABLE_UNDER_REPERTOIRE', 'INDISTINGUISHABLE_UNDER_REPERTOIRE', 'INDISTINGUISHABLE_UNDER_REPERTOIRE', 'INDISTINGUISHABLE_UNDER_REPERTOIRE']  -- and the droplet arm reports them as an EQUIVALENCE CLASS, not as the same system

cases.case: C2 FALSE SAMENESS INCREASES as experimental access gets poorer (measured, not assumed)
E1 function-preserving REPLACEMENT during one continuous trajectory: behaviour uninterrupted
cases.detail: identical before the swap; a bounded transient of 14 steps; uninterrupted from t=54

cases.case: E1 the fingerprint SURVIVES the replacement (pre/post distance inside the continuity radius)
cases.detail: pre-replacement vs post-replacement fingerprint distance = 0.0000 (continuity radius 0.015)

cases.case: L1 MUST-FAIL: re-adding the class label and lag set RECREATES the false difference (D-069)
cases.detail: clean d=0.0000 (INDISTINGUISHABLE) -> contaminated d=0.0612 (DIFFERENT). The exclusion of description-level quantities is LOAD-BEARING, not decoration.

cases.case: L2 MUST-FAIL: removing the discriminating probe COLLAPSES two different systems into one fingerprint
cases.detail: with the register step d=0.0347 (DIFFERENT under rich) -> without it d=0.0000 (INDISTINGUISHABLE). PROBE-REPERTOIRE ADEQUACY IS LOAD-BEARING: the difference was real and the poorer repertoire cannot see it.
